_ocr_image_bytes = ocr_image_bytes # alias for internal callers 8: 9: SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS = {".pdf", ".docx", ".pptx"} 10: 11: 12: def detect_file_type(filename: str) -> str | None: 13: ext = Path(filename).suffix.lower() 14: if ext == ".pdf": 15: return "pdf" 16: elif ext == ".docx": 17: return "docx" 18: elif ext == ".pptx": 19: return "pptx" 20: return None 21: 22: 23: def ocr_image_file(file_path: str) -> str: 24: with open(file_path, "rb") as f: 25: return ocr_image_bytes(f.read()) 26: 27: 28: def convert_to_pdf(input_path: str, output_dir: str) -> str: 29: import comtypes.client 30: import pythoncom 31: 32: pythoncom.CoInitialize() 33: 34: src = Path(input_path).resolve() 35: out = Path(output_dir).resolve() / f"{src.stem}.pdf" 36: ext = src.suffix.lower() 37: 38: try: 39: if ext == ".docx": 40: app = comtypes.client.CreateObject("Word.Application") 41: app.Visible = False 42: doc = app.Documents.Open(str(src)) 43: doc.ExportAsFixedFormat(str(out), 17) 44: doc.Close() 45: app.Quit() 46: 47: elif ext == ".pptx": 48: app = comtypes.client.CreateObject("PowerPoint.Application") 49: app.Visible = False 50: pres = app.Presentations.Open(str(src)) 51: pres.SaveAs(str(out), 32) 52: pres.Close() 53: app.Quit() 54: 55: else: 56: raise ValueError(f"Unsupported for COM conversion: {ext}") 57: 58: finally: 59: pythoncom.CoUninitialize() 60: 61: return str(out) 62: 63: 64: def _extract_pdf(path: str) -> list[dict]: 65: try: 66: text = ocr_pdf_file(path) 67: if text.strip(): 68: return [{"page": 1, "source": "gemini", "text": text}] 69: except Exception: 70: pass 71: import fitz 72: doc = fitz.open(path) 73: try: 74: pages = [] 75: for page_num, page in enumerate(doc): 76: native_text = page.get_text().strip() 77: pix = page.get_pixmap(dpi=200) 78: img_bytes = pix.tobytes("png") 79: ocr_text = _ocr_image_bytes(img_bytes) 80: combined = f"{native_text}
{ocr_text}".strip() 81: source = "native+ocr" if native_text and ocr_text else ("ocr" if ocr_text else "native") 82: pages.append({ 83: "page": page_num + 1, 84: "source": source, 85: "text": combined, 86: }) 87: return pages 88: finally: 89: doc.close() 90: 91: 92: def _try_gemini_pdf(path: str) -> str | None: 93: with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir: 94: try: 95: pdf_path = convert_to_pdf(path, tmpdir) 96: text = ocr_pdf_file(pdf_path) 97: if text.strip(): 98: return text 99: except Exception: 100: pass 101: return None 102: 103: 104: def _extract_docx(path: str) -> list[dict]: 105: text = _try_gemini_pdf(path) 106: if text is not None: 107: return [{"page": 1, "source": "gemini", "text": text}] 108: from docx import Document 109: doc = Document(path) 110: paragraphs = [p.text for p in doc.paragraphs] 111: native_text = "
".join(paragraphs).strip() 112: pages = [{"page": 1, "source": "native", "text": native_text}] 113: ocr_parts = [] 114: for rel in doc.part.rels.values(): 115: if "image" in rel.reltype: 116: image_data = rel.target_part.blob 117: text = _ocr_image_bytes(image_data) 118: if text.strip(): 119: ocr_parts.append(text) 120: if ocr_parts: 121: pages.append({"page": 1, "source": "native+ocr", "text": "
".join(ocr_parts)}) 122: return pages 123: 124: 125: def _extract_pptx(path: str) -> list[dict]: 126: text = _try_gemini_pdf(path) 127: if text is not None: 128: return [{"page": 1, "source": "gemini", "text": text}] 129: from pptx import Presentation 130: from pptx.enum.shapes import MSO_SHAPE_TYPE 131: prs = Presentation(path) 132: slides_data = [] 133: for slide_num, slide in enumerate(prs.slides): 134: texts = [] 135: for shape in slide.shapes: 136: if shape.has_text_frame and shape.text_frame.text.strip(): 137: texts.append(shape.text_frame.text) 138: if shape.shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.PICTURE: 139: img_data = shape.image.blob 140: ocr_text = _ocr_image_bytes(img_data) 141: if ocr_text.strip(): 142: texts.append(ocr_text) 143: slides_data.append({ 144: "page": slide_num + 1, 145: "source": "native", 146: "text": "
".join(texts), 147: }) 148: return slides_data 149: 150: 151: def process_document( 152: file_path: str, 153: chat_id: int, 154: message_id: int, 155: sender_name: str, 156: sent_at: datetime, 157: chat_title: str | None = None, 158: ) -> dict: 159: path = Path(file_path) 160: file_type = detect_file_type(path.name) 161: 162: if file_type == "pdf": 163: pages = _extract_pdf(file_path) 164: elif file_type == "docx": 165: pages = _extract_docx(file_path) 166: elif file_type == "pptx": 167: pages = _extract_pptx(file_path) 168: else: 169: raise ValueError(f"Unsupported file type: {file_type}") 170: 171: full_text = "

" + "=" * 70) 19: print(title) 20: print("=" * 70) 21: 22: 23: def run(): 24: section("P0: Custom FailurePolicy — TECHNICAL retries 3x instead of default 1x") 25: 26: tasks = [{"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "IP Setup", "dependencies": []}] 27: engine = SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 28: engine.tasks["T01"]["assigned_to"] = "Zul_IT_Tech" 29: 30: custom_policies = { 31: "RESOURCE": FailurePolicy("RESOURCE", "procurement_lead", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=8.0), 32: "SITE": FailurePolicy("SITE", "project_manager", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=10.0), 33: "TECHNICAL": FailurePolicy("TECHNICAL", "senior_network_lead", max_local_retries=3, urgency_weight=20.0), 34: "QUALITY": FailurePolicy("QUALITY", "site_supervisor", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=5.0), 35: } 36: 37: solver = VRPSolver(engine, {"Zul_IT_Tech": {"skills": [], "lat": 0, "lng": 0}}) 38: notifier = StubNotifier() 39: dispatcher = FieldOpsDispatcher(engine, solver, notifier, policies=custom_policies) 40: 41: engine.update_task_state("T01", "ACTIVE", "Zul starts config") 42: 43: actions = [] 44: for attempt in range(1, 5): 45: decision = dispatcher.process_failure_report("T01", "IP_CONFLICT", "Zul_IT_Tech") 46: actions.append(decision["action"]) 47: print(f"Attempt {attempt}: action={decision['action']}, " 48: f"escalate_to_role={decision.get('escalate_to_role', 'n/a')}") 49: if decision["action"] == "ESCALATE": 50: break 51: engine.tasks["T01"]["state"] = "ACTIVE" 52: 53: print(f"
Action sequence: {actions}") 54: expected = ["RETRY_LOCAL", "RETRY_LOCAL", "RETRY_LOCAL", "ESCALATE"] 55: retries_ok = actions == expected 56: print(f"
>>> Retry count honored custom policy (expected {expected}): {'PASS' if retries_ok else 'FAIL'}") 57: 58: return {"retries_ok": retries_ok, "actions": actions} 59: 60: 61: def run_role_check(): 62: section("P0 (continued): verify escalation role comes from the CUSTOM policy") 63: 64: tasks = [{"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "IP Setup", "dependencies": []}] 65: engine = SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 66: engine.tasks["T01"]["assigned_to"] = "Zul_IT_Tech" 67: 68: custom_policies = { 69: "RESOURCE": FailurePolicy("RESOURCE", "procurement_lead", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=8.0), 70: "SITE": FailurePolicy("SITE", "project_manager", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=10.0), 71: "TECHNICAL": FailurePolicy("TECHNICAL", "senior_network_lead", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=20.0), 72: "QUALITY": FailurePolicy("QUALITY", "site_supervisor", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=5.0), 73: } 74: 75: solver = VRPSolver(engine, {"Zul_IT_Tech": {"skills": [], "lat": 0, "lng": 0}}) 76: dispatcher = FieldOpsDispatcher(engine, solver, StubNotifier(), policies=custom_policies) 77: 78: engine.update_task_state("T01", "ACTIVE") 79: decision = dispatcher.process_failure_report("T01", "IP_CONFLICT", "Zul_IT_Tech") 80: 81: print(f"Action: {decision['action']}") 82: print(f"Escalated to: {decision.get('escalate_to_role')}") 83: 84: expected_role = "senior_network_lead" 85: role_ok = decision.get("escalate_to_role") == expected_role 86: immediate_escalate_ok = decision["action"] == "ESCALATE" 87: 88: print(f"

" + "#" * 70) 100: all_pass = r1["retries_ok"] and r2["role_ok"] and r2["immediate_escalate_ok"] 101: print(f"P0 CUSTOM FAILURE POLICY: {'ALL PASS' if all_pass else 'FAILURES FOUND'}") 102: print("#" * 70) 1: """ 2: P0 — Reverse cascade: COMPLETE -> REGRESSED propagation through descendants 3: that are themselves already COMPLETE. 4: 5: This is the test the user correctly identified as missing and as the single 6: most important one: every cascade test so far drove failures FORWARD from 7: a node that hadn't finished yet. This test instead completes an ENTIRE 8: multi-layer chain first, then regresses a node in the MIDDLE, and checks 9: that every downstream node that was COMPLETE flips to REGRESSED -- which is 10: the actual claim behind "bidirectional DAG." 11: 12: Run: PYTHONPATH=. python -m backend.tests.test_p0_regression_cascade 13: """ 14: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import SyncFieldDAG 15: 16: 17: def section(title): 18: print("
" + "=" * 70) 19: print(title) 20: print("=" * 70) 21: 22: 23: def build_chain(): 24: tasks = [ 25: {"task_id": "A", "task_name": "Survey", "dependencies": []}, 26: {"task_id": "B", "task_name": "Conduit", "dependencies": ["A"]}, 27: {"task_id": "C", "task_name": "CableRun", "dependencies": ["B"]}, 28: {"task_id": "D", "task_name": "RackSetup", "dependencies": ["B"]}, 29: {"task_id": "E", "task_name": "Termination", "dependencies": ["C", "D"]}, 30: ] 31: return SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 32: 33: 34: def run(): 35: section("P0 TEST 1: Full completion, then regress B mid-chain") 36: 37: engine = build_chain() 38: for t_id in ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E"]: 39: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "ACTIVE") 40: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "COMPLETE") 41: 42: print("
All tasks COMPLETE. States:") 43: for t_id in ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E"]: 44: print(f" {t_id}: {engine.tasks[t_id]['state']}") 45: 46: print("
Now regressing B (e.g. inspection finds the conduit was never actually installed):") 47: events = engine.update_task_state("B", "REGRESSED", "Inspection found conduit missing") 48: 49: print(f"
{len(events)} events generated:") 50: for e in events: 51: print(f" depth={e.get('depth')} | {e['task_id']:<3} | {e['old_state']:<10} -> {e['new_state']:<10} | {e['reason']}") 52: 53: print("
Final states:") 54: failures = [] 55: expected = {"A": "COMPLETE", "B": "REGRESSED", "C": "REGRESSED", "D": "REGRESSED", "E": "REGRESSED"} 56: for t_id, exp in expected.items(): 57: actual = engine.tasks[t_id]["state"] 58: status = "OK" if actual == exp else "FAIL" 59: if status == "FAIL": 60: failures.append(t_id) 61: print(f" {t_id}: {actual} (expected {exp}) [{status}]") 62: 63: if failures: 64: print(f"
>>> P0 FAILURE: nodes {failures} did not regress correctly.") 65: print(">>> This means 'bidirectional DAG' is not actually proven for multi-layer chains.") 66: else: 67: print("
>>> P0 PASS: COMPLETE descendants correctly cascade to REGRESSED, " 68: "multiple layers deep, through a multi-parent join (E depends on both C and D).") 69: 70: return {"failures": failures} 71: 72: 73: def run_partial_regression(): 74: section("P0 TEST 2: Partial regression — only one of two parents regresses") 75: 76: engine = build_chain() 77: for t_id in ["A", "B", "C", "D", "E"]: 78: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "ACTIVE") 79: engine.update_task_state(t_id, "COMPLETE") 80: 81: print("Regressing C only (D stays COMPLETE). E depends on both C AND D.") 82: engine.update_task_state("C", "REGRESSED", "Cable damaged, found during QA") 83: 84: print(f"
D state (should remain COMPLETE, untouched): {engine.tasks['D']['state']}") 85: print(f"E state (should regress, since C is one of its deps): {engine.tasks['E']['state']}") 86: 87: ok = engine.tasks["D"]["state"] == "COMPLETE" and engine.tasks["E"]["state"] == "REGRESSED" 88: print(f"
>>> {'PASS' if ok else 'FAIL'}: partial-parent regression correctly handled = {ok}") 89: return {"pass": ok} 90: 91: 92: if __name__ == "__main__": 93: r1 = run() 94: r2 = run_partial_regression() 95: print("

NEVER guess. 6: Output task_name in English. Return ONLY valid JSON. 7: 8: Schema: 9: { 10: "label": "task_completion"|"task_start"|"material_request"|"issue_report"|"progress_update"|"instruction"|"unclear"|"other", 11: "task_name": str|null, 12: "status": "done"|"in_progress"|"blocked"|null, 13: "material": str|null, 14: "quantity": float|null, 15: "confidence": "high"|"low", 16: "note": str|null 17: } 18: 19: Example: 20: Input: 21: Valid tasks: [foundation, framing] 22: Chat: Site A Crew 23: Sender: Ali 24: Time: 2026-06-20T14:30:00+00:00 25: Type: text 26: Message replied to: "Ali please finish the foundation today" 27: Message: "done" 28: Output: 29: {"label":"task_completion","task_name":"foundation","status":"done","material":null,"quantity":null,"confidence":"high","note":null}""" 1: """ 2: P1 — Full Schedule Engine pipeline: AvailabilityWindow + TechnicianSchedule + 3: compute_cascade_eta(schedules=...) actually wired together. 4: 5: Run: PYTHONPATH=. python -m backend.tests.test_p1_schedule_pipeline 6: """ 7: from datetime import datetime, timedelta 8: 9: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import ( 10: SyncFieldDAG, AvailabilityWindow, TechnicianSchedule, 11: ) 12: 13: 14: def section(title): 15: print("

>>> {'PASS' if expected_shift else 'FAIL'}: Afternoon window shifted from 13:00 to 14:30") 67: 68: section("P1 TEST 2c: delay_from() on a SINGLE large window — mid-window split") 69: schedule3 = TechnicianSchedule([ 70: AvailabilityWindow(datetime(2026, 6, 22, 8, 0), datetime(2026, 6, 22, 18, 0), source="shift", confirmed=True), 71: ]) 72: schedule3.delay_from(datetime(2026, 6, 22, 10, 0), by=timedelta(minutes=90), confirmed=False) 73: 74: print("Single 08:00-18:00 window. >>> {'PASS' if ok else 'FAIL'}: T02 pushed past Ahmad's lunch to 14:00") 123: return {"pass": ok} 124: 125: 126: if __name__ == "__main__": 127: r1 = run_basic_window_removal() 128: r2 = run_delay_from() 129: r3 = run_eta_with_real_schedule() 130: print("
" + "#" * 70) 131: all_pass = r1["pass"] and r2["pass"] and r3["pass"] 132: print(f"P1 SCHEDULE PIPELINE: {'ALL PASS' if all_pass else 'FAILURES FOUND'}") 133: print("#" * 70) 1: """ 2: P2 — Cyclic dependencies and FAILED-state re-report. 3: 4: Run: PYTHONPATH=. python -m backend.tests.test_p2_edge_cases 5: """ 6: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import SyncFieldDAG, DEFAULT_POLICIES 7: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import FieldOpsDomainRules 8: 9: 10: def section(title): 11: print("
" + "=" * 70) 12: print(title) 13: print("=" * 70) 14: 15: 16: def run_cyclic_dependency(): 17: section("P2 TEST 1: Cyclic dependency — T01 depends on T02, T02 depends on T01") 18: 19: tasks = [ 20: {"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "A", "dependencies": ["T02"]}, 21: {"task_id": "T02", "task_name": "B", "dependencies": ["T01"]}, 22: ] 23: 24: print("Constructing a 2-node cycle. Unexpectedly SUCCEEDED in {elapsed:.3f}s — silent deadlock accepted.") 32: print(f"T01 state: {engine.tasks['T01']['state']}") 33: print(f"T02 state: {engine.tasks['T02']['state']}") 34: return {"hung": False, "rejected": False} 35: except ValueError as e: 36: elapsed = time.time() - start 37: print(f"
Correctly rejected in {elapsed:.3f}s: {e}") 38: return {"hung": False, "rejected": True} 39: 40: 41: def run_failed_task_reporting_failure(): 42: section("P2 TEST 2: A FAILED (terminal) task receives another failure report") 43: 44: tasks = [{"task_id": "T01", "task_name": "Install", "dependencies": []}] 45: engine = SyncFieldDAG(tasks) 46: engine.tasks["T01"]["assigned_to"] = "Ahmad" 47: 48: engine.update_task_state("T01", "ACTIVE") 49: engine.update_task_state("T01", "BLOCKED", "first failure") 50: engine.update_task_state("T01", "FAILED", "abandoned after no fix possible") 51: print(f"T01 is now FAILED (terminal state).") 52: 53: domain_rules = FieldOpsDomainRules(DEFAULT_POLICIES) 54: 55: print("
Now a technician reports ANOTHER failure against the same (FAILED) task:") 56: decision = domain_rules.handle_task_failure(engine, "T01", "MATERIAL_MISSING", "Ahmad") 57: print(f"Result: action={decision['action']}, msg={decision['msg']}") 58: print(f"T01 state after: {engine.tasks['T01']['state']} (unchanged)") 59: 60: guarded = decision["action"] == "ALREADY_FAILED" and engine.tasks["T01"]["state"] == "FAILED" 61: print(f"
>>> {'PASS' if guarded else 'FAIL'}: ALREADY_FAILED guard correctly no-ops without exception") 62: return {"crashed": False, "guarded": guarded} 63: 64: 65: if __name__ == "__main__": 66: r1 = run_cyclic_dependency() 67: r2 = run_failed_task_reporting_failure() 68: 69: print("
" + "#" * 70) 70: print("P2 EDGE CASE SUMMARY") 71: print("#" * 70) 72: print(f"2-node cycle correctly rejected at construction: {r1['rejected']}") 73: print(f"FAILED re-report guarded with ALREADY_FAILED: {r2['guarded']}") 1: # backend/workflow/event_handlers/event_bus.py 2: 3: import asyncio 4: from supabase import Client 5: from backend.workflow.event_handlers.dispatcher import FieldOpsDispatcher 6: 7: 8: class SupabaseEventBus: 9: """ 10: Coordinates real-time Postgres mutations with the running 11: FieldOpsDispatcher and SyncFieldDAG engine. 12: 13: Single-direction: Supabase → DAG Engine. 14: Writes back via sync_to_supabase() with idempotency guard. 15: """ 16: 17: def __init__(self, dispatcher: FieldOpsDispatcher, sb_client: Client): 18: self.dispatcher = dispatcher 19: self.sb = sb_client 20: 21: def handle_db_change(self, payload: dict) -> None: 22: record = payload.get("new") 23: if not record: 24: return 25: 26: task_id = record.get("id") 27: db_state = record.get("state") 28: 29: if not task_id or not db_state: 30: return 31: 32: task = self.dispatcher.engine.tasks.get(task_id) 33: if not task: 34: return 35: 36: current_memory_state = task["state"] 37: if current_memory_state == db_state: 38: return 39: 40: print(f"[EventBus] External change: {task_id} {current_memory_state} → {db_state}") 41: 42: try: 43: events = self.dispatcher.engine.update_task_state( 44: task_id, 45: db_state, 46: f"SyncField Event Bus: Synchronized from Supabase" 47: ) 48: if events: 49: self.dispatcher.engine.sync_to_supabase(events, self.sb) 50: except Exception as e: 51: print(f"[EventBus] Error processing {task_id}: {e}") 52: 53: 54: class RealtimeEventBusListener: 55: """ 56: Subscribes to Supabase Realtime WebSocket CDC streams and routes 57: payloads into the active Event Bus router. 58: 59: Uses the async Supabase client because realtime-py only supports async. 60: """ 61: 62: def __init__(self, bus: SupabaseEventBus, async_sb=None): 63: self.bus = bus 64: self.async_sb = async_sb 65: self.channel = None 66: 67: def _on_postgres_change(self, payload: dict) -> None: 68: self.bus.handle_db_change(payload) 69: 70: async def start(self) -> None: 71: self.channel = self.async_sb.channel("tasks_realtime_sync") 72: self.channel.on( 73: "postgres_changes", 74: {"event": "UPDATE", "schema": "public", "table": "tasks"}, 75: self._on_postgres_change, 76: ) 77: await self.channel.subscribe() 78: print("[EventBus] Subscribed to Supabase Realtime on table 'tasks'") 79: 80: def start_sync(self) -> None: 81: asyncio.create_task(self._start_wrapped()) 82: 83: async def _start_wrapped(self) -> None: 84: try: 85: await self.start() 86: except Exception as e: 87: print(f"[EventBus] Subscription error: {e}") 88: 89: async def stop(self) -> None: 90: if self.channel: 91: await self.async_sb.remove_channel(self.channel) 92: self.channel = None 93: print("[EventBus] Unsubscribed from Realtime") 1: # backend/workflow/template_guesser.py 2: 3: from dataclasses import dataclass, field 4: from datetime import datetime, timedelta 5: from typing import Dict, List, Set 6: 7: 8: @dataclass 9: class GuessedDependency: 10: parent_name: str 11: confidence: float 12: evidence: str 13: 14: 15: @dataclass 16: class GuessedTask: 17: task_name: str 18: suggested_dependencies: List[GuessedDependency] = field(default_factory=list) 19: guessed: bool = False 20: 21: 22: class TemplateGuesser: 23: def __init__(self, sb_client): 24: self.sb = sb_client 25: 26: def guess_template(self, new_task_names: List[str]) -> Dict[str, GuessedTask]: 27: if not new_task_names: 28: return {} 29: 30: try: 31: res = ( 32: self.sb.table("tasks") 33: .select("project_id, task_name, id, dependencies") 34: .execute() 35: ) 36: raw_tasks = res.data or [] 37: except Exception: 38: raw_tasks = [] 39: 40: if not raw_tasks: 41: return { 42: name: GuessedTask( 43: task_name=name, 44: suggested_dependencies=[], 45: guessed=True, 46: ) 47: for name in new_task_names 48: } 49: 50: project_id_to_id_map: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {} 51: project_tasks: Dict[str, List[dict]] = {} 52: 53: for t in raw_tasks: 54: pid = t.get("project_id") 55: if not pid: 56: continue 57: project_id_to_id_map.setdefault(pid, {})[t["id"]] = t["task_name"] 58: project_tasks.setdefault(pid, []).append(t) 59: 60: dep_counts: Dict[str, Dict[str, int]] = {} 61: task_project_counts: Dict[str, int] = {} 62: 63: for pid, t_list in project_tasks.items(): 64: id_name_map = project_id_to_id_map[pid] 65: seen_in_project: Set[str] = set() 66: 67: for t in t_list: 68: b_name = t["task_name"] 69: if b_name in seen_in_project: 70: continue 71: seen_in_project.add(b_name) 72: 73: task_project_counts[b_name] = task_project_counts.get(b_name, 0) + 1 74: 75: deps = t.get("dependencies") or [] 76: for dep_id in deps: 77: parent_name = id_name_map.get(dep_id) 78: if parent_name: 79: if b_name not in dep_counts: 80: dep_counts[b_name] = {} 81: dep_counts[b_name][parent_name] = ( 82: dep_counts[b_name].get(parent_name, 0) + 1 83: ) 84: 85: guesses: Dict[str, GuessedTask] = {} 86: for target_name in new_task_names: 87: guessed_task = GuessedTask(task_name=target_name, guessed=False) 88: total_b = task_project_counts.get(target_name, 0) 89: 90: if total_b == 0: 91: guessed_task.guessed = True 92: guesses[target_name] = guessed_task 93: continue 94: 95: parents = dep_counts.get(target_name, {}) 96: for parent_name, count in parents.items(): 97: if parent_name not in new_task_names: 98: continue 99: 100: confidence = round(count / total_b, 2) 101: guessed_task.suggested_dependencies.append( 102: GuessedDependency( 103: parent_name=parent_name, 104: confidence=confidence, 105: evidence=f"Seen in {count}/{total_b} historical projects", 106: ) 107: ) 108: 109: guessed_task.suggested_dependencies.sort( 110: key=lambda x: x.confidence, reverse=True 111: ) 112: guesses[target_name] = guessed_task 113: 114: return guesses 115: 116: @staticmethod 117: def instantiate( 118: guesses: Dict[str, GuessedTask], 119: project_id: str, 120: site_prefix: str, 121: confidence_threshold: float = 0.5, 122: lat: float = 3.120, 123: lng: float = 101.655, 124: ) -> List[dict]: 125: sorted_names = sorted(guesses.keys()) 126: name_to_prefixed_id: Dict[str, str] = {} 127: for i, name in enumerate(sorted_names): 128: name_to_prefixed_id[name] = f"{site_prefix}_T{i+1:02d}" 129: 130: instantiated: List[dict] = [] 131: base_time = datetime.now() 132: 133: for name, g_task in guesses.items(): 134: assigned_id = name_to_prefixed_id[name] 135: resolved_deps: List[str] = [] 136: 137: for dep in g_task.suggested_dependencies: 138: if dep.confidence >= confidence_threshold: 139: parent_id = name_to_prefixed_id.get(dep.parent_name) 140: if parent_id and parent_id != assigned_id: 141: resolved_deps.append(parent_id) 142: 143: instantiated.append( 144: { 145: "task_id": assigned_id, 146: "project_id": project_id, 147: "task_name": name, 148: "dependencies": sorted(resolved_deps), 149: "deadline": base_time + timedelta(days=7), 150: "lat": lat, 151: "lng": lng, 152: "estimated_duration_hours": 3, 153: "state": "LOCKED", 154: "attempt_count": 0, 155: "failure_category": None, 156: "assigned_to": None, 157: } 158: ) 159: 160: return instantiated 161: 162: def save_template(self, name: str, description: str, guesses: Dict[str, GuessedTask]) -> dict: 163: serialized_tasks = [] 164: for task_name, g_task in guesses.items(): 165: serialized_tasks.append({ 166: "task_name": task_name, 167: "suggested_dependencies": [ 168: { 169: "parent_name": dep.parent_name, 170: "confidence": dep.confidence, 171: "evidence": dep.evidence, 172: } 173: for dep in g_task.suggested_dependencies 174: ], 175: "guessed": g_task.guessed, 176: }) 177: 178: payload = { 179: "name": name, 180: "description": description, 181: "tasks": serialized_tasks, 182: "updated_at": "now()", 183: } 184: 185: res = self.sb.table("project_templates").upsert(payload).execute() 186: return res.data[0] if res.data else {} 187: 188: def load_template(self, name: str) -> Dict[str, GuessedTask]: 189: res = self.sb.table("project_templates").select("tasks").eq("name", name).maybe_single().execute() 190: if not res or not res.data: 191: raise ValueError(f"Project template '{name}' not found.") 192: 193: raw_tasks = res.data.get("tasks", []) 194: guesses: Dict[str, GuessedTask] = {} 195: for t in raw_tasks: 196: task_name = t["task_name"] 197: guesses[task_name] = GuessedTask( 198: task_name=task_name, 199: suggested_dependencies=[ 200: GuessedDependency(**dep) 201: for dep in t.get("suggested_dependencies", []) 202: ], 203: guessed=t.get("guessed", False), 204: ) 205: return guesses 1: -- ============================================ 2: -- CORE ENTITIES — Field Operations DAG 3: -- ============================================ 4: 5: -- Sites (buildings/locations) 6: create table if not exists sites ( 7: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 8: name text not null, 9: address text, 10: lat double precision, 11: lng double precision, 12: power_ready boolean default true, 13: access_status text default 'open', 14: created_at timestamptz default now() 15: ); 16: 17: -- Projects (a site can have multiple projects, typically 1:1) 18: create table if not exists projects ( 19: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 20: name text not null, 21: site_id uuid references sites(id), 22: status text default 'active', 23: created_at timestamptz default now() 24: ); 25: 26: -- Technicians (field staff) 27: create table if not exists technicians ( 28: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 29: name text not null, 30: phone text, 31: telegram_id text, 32: role text default 'technician', 33: skills text[] default '{}', 34: lat double precision, 35: lng double precision, 36: shift_start time default '08:00', 37: shift_end time default '18:00', 38: van_inventory jsonb default '{}', 39: status text default 'available', 40: created_at timestamptz default now() 41: ); 42: 43: -- Tasks = DAG nodes. IDs are text matching Python ("T01", "T02"). 44: create table if not exists tasks ( 45: id text primary key, 46: project_id uuid references projects(id) on delete cascade, 47: task_name text not null, 48: state text not null default 'LOCKED', 49: dependencies text[] default '{}', 50: assigned_to uuid references technicians(id), 51: failure_category text, 52: attempt_count int default 0, 53: blocked_since timestamptz, 54: scheduled_start timestamptz, 55: deadline timestamptz, 56: required_skills text[], 57: requires_shared_tool boolean default false, 58: created_at timestamptz default now(), 59: updated_at timestamptz default now() 60: ); 61: 62: -- Original messages (raw, exactly what came in from Telegram, before any LLM analysis) 63: create table if not exists original_messages ( 64: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 65: telegram_id text, 66: sender_name text, 67: chat_title text, 68: sent_at timestamptz, 69: type text, 70: text text not null, 71: replied_to text, 72: created_at timestamptz default now() 73: ); 74: 75: -- Processed messages (Tier 1/2/3 resolution audit trail — the LLM's classification of one original_messages row) 76: create table if not exists processed_messages ( 77: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 78: original_message_id uuid references original_messages(id) on delete cascade, 79: technician_id uuid references technicians(id), 80: task_id text references tasks(id), 81: label text, 82: status text, 83: material text, 84: quantity numeric, 85: resolved_state text, 86: resolved_failure_type text, 87: tier_resolved text, 88: confidence numeric, 89: note text, 90: created_at timestamptz default now() 91: ); 92: 93: -- Task events (state transitions — drives cascade animation) 94: create table if not exists task_events ( 95: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 96: task_id text references tasks(id), 97: old_state text, 98: new_state text, 99: reason text, 100: triggered_by text, 101: created_at timestamptz default now() 102: ); 103: 104: -- Alerts (fired by routing/dispatcher layer) 105: create table if not exists alerts ( 106: id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), 107: task_id text references tasks(id), 108: target_role text, 109: target_technician_id uuid references technicians(id), 110: category text, 111: message text, 112: status text default 'pending', 113: created_at timestamptz default now() 114: ); 115: 116: -- Resolution cache (exact-match LLM cost optimization) 117: create table if not exists resolution_cache ( 118: cache_key text primary key, 119: resolved_state text, 120: resolved_failure_type text, 121: hit_count int default 1, 122: created_at timestamptz default now() 123: ); 124: 125: -- ============================================ 126: -- SUPABASE REALTIME 127: -- ============================================ 128: do $$ 129: begin 130: if not exists ( 131: select 1 from pg_publication_tables 132: where pubname = 'supabase_realtime' and tablename = 'tasks' 133: ) then 134: alter publication supabase_realtime add table tasks; 135: end if; 136: if not exists ( 137: select 1 from pg_publication_tables 138: where pubname = 'supabase_realtime' and tablename = 'task_events' 139: ) then 140: alter publication supabase_realtime add table task_events; 141: end if; 142: if not exists ( 143: select 1 from pg_publication_tables 144: where pubname = 'supabase_realtime' and tablename = 'alerts' 145: ) then 146: alter publication supabase_realtime add table alerts; 147: end if; 148: if not exists ( 149: select 1 from pg_publication_tables 150: where pubname = 'supabase_realtime' and tablename = 'processed_messages' 151: ) then 152: alter publication supabase_realtime add table processed_messages; 153: end if; 154: end $$; 155: 156: -- ============================================ 157: -- INDEXES 158: -- ============================================ 159: create index if not exists idx_tasks_project_id on tasks(project_id); 160: create index if not exists idx_tasks_state on tasks(state); 161: create index if not exists idx_task_events_task_id on task_events(task_id); 162: create index if not exists idx_task_events_created_at on task_events(created_at desc); 163: create index if not exists idx_alerts_status on alerts(status); 164: create index if not exists idx_original_messages_created_at on original_messages(created_at desc); 165: create index if not exists idx_processed_messages_original_message_id on processed_messages(original_message_id); 166: create index if not exists idx_processed_messages_task_id on processed_messages(task_id); 1: import asyncio 2: from datetime import datetime 3: from pathlib import Path 4: import json 5: 6: from telethon import TelegramClient, Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / "downloads" 16: 17: 18: async def _analyze_and_log(message: dict, telegram_id) -> None: 19: try: 20: await asyncio.to_thread(process_message, message, telegram_id) 21: except Exception as e: 22: print(json.dumps({**message, "error": str(e)}, ensure_ascii=False)) 23: 24: 25: async def setup_listener(client: TelegramClient) -> None: 26: DOWNLOAD_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True) 27: preload_model() 28: 29: @client.on(events.NewMessage) 30: async def handler(event) -> None: 31: sender = await event.get_sender() 32: name = get_display_name(sender) if sender else "Unknown" 33: telegram_id = sender.id if sender else None 34: sent_at_iso = event.date.isoformat() 35: chat_title = getattr(event.chat, "title", None) or "Private" 36: 37: if event.message.voice: 38: ts_file = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S") 39: tmp = DOWNLOAD_DIR / f"voice_{event.chat_id}_{ts_file}.ogg" 40: await client.download_media(event.message, file=str(tmp)) 41: try: 42: english_text = translate_audio(str(tmp)) 43: message = {"sender_name": name, "sent_at": sent_at_iso, "chat_title": chat_title, "type": "voice", "text": english_text} 44: await _analyze_and_log(message, telegram_id) 45: except Exception as e: 46: print(json.dumps({"sender_name": name, "sent_at": sent_at_iso, "chat_title": chat_title, "type": "voice", "error": str(e)}, ensure_ascii=False)) 47: finally: 48: tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True) 49: return 50: 51: if event.message.document: 52: doc = event.message.document 53: filename = None 54: for attr in doc.attributes: 55: if isinstance(attr, DocumentAttributeFilename): 56: filename = attr.file_name 57: break 58: if filename and detect_file_type(filename): 59: ts_file = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S") 60: tmp = DOWNLOAD_DIR / f"doc_{event.chat_id}_{ts_file}{Path(filename).suffix}" 61: await client.download_media(event.message, file=str(tmp)) 62: get_queue().enqueue(str(tmp), event.chat_id, event.message.id, 63: name, event.date, chat_title, telegram_id) 64: return 65: 66: if event.text: 67: message = {"sender_name": name, "sent_at": sent_at_iso, "chat_title": chat_title, "type": "text", "text": event.text} 68: await _analyze_and_log(message, telegram_id) 69: 70: if event.photo: 71: ts_file = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S") 72: fname = f"photo_{event.chat_id}_{ts_file}.jpg" 73: photo_path = DOWNLOAD_DIR / fname 74: await client.download_media(event.message, file=str(photo_path)) 75: try: 76: ocr_text = ocr_image_file(str(photo_path)) 77: if ocr_text.strip(): 78: message = {"sender_name": name, "sent_at": sent_at_iso, "chat_title": chat_title, "type": "photo", "text": ocr_text} 79: await _analyze_and_log(message, telegram_id) 80: except Exception as e: 81: print(json.dumps({"sender_name": name, "sent_at": sent_at_iso, "chat_title": chat_title, "type": "photo", "error": str(e)}, ensure_ascii=False)) 82: finally: 83: photo_path.unlink(missing_ok=True) 84: 85: print("Listening for new messages...") 1: import asyncio 2: import json 3: from datetime import datetime 4: from pathlib import Path 5: 6: from .document_processor import process_document 7: from backend.llm.pipeline import process_message 8: 9: 10: class OcrQueue: 11: def __init__(self) -> None: 12: self._tasks: set[asyncio.Task] = set() 13: 14: def enqueue( 15: self, 16: file_path: str, 17: chat_id: int, 18: message_id: int, 19: sender_name: str, 20: sent_at: datetime, 21: chat_title: str | None = None, 22: telegram_id=None, 23: ) -> None: 24: task = asyncio.create_task( 25: self._run(file_path, chat_id, message_id, sender_name, sent_at, chat_title, telegram_id) 26: ) 27: self._tasks.add(task) 28: task.add_done_callback(self._tasks.discard) 29: 30: async def _run( 31: self, 32: file_path: str, 33: chat_id: int, 34: message_id: int, 35: sender_name: str, 36: sent_at: datetime, 37: chat_title: str | None = None, 38: telegram_id=None, 39: ) -> None: 40: path = Path(file_path) 41: try: 42: result = process_document(file_path, chat_id, message_id, sender_name, sent_at, chat_title) 43: sent_at_str = sent_at.isoformat() if hasattr(sent_at, "isoformat") else str(sent_at) 44: message = {"sender_name": sender_name, "sent_at": sent_at_str, "chat_title": chat_title, "type": "document", "text": result["full_text"]} 45: await asyncio.to_thread(process_message, message, telegram_id) 46: except Exception as e: 47: print(json.dumps({"sender_name": sender_name, "type": "document", "error": str(e)}, ensure_ascii=False)) 48: finally: 49: path.unlink(missing_ok=True) 50: 51: 52: _queue: OcrQueue | None = None 53: 54: 55: def get_queue() -> OcrQueue: 56: global _queue 57: if _queue is None: 58: _queue = OcrQueue() 59: return _queue 1: from backend.integrations.supabase.client import get_supabase_client 2: from backend.llm.analyzer import llm_analyze 3: 4: CONFIDENCE_MAP = {"high": 1.0, "low": 0.5} 5: 6: _dispatcher = None 7: 8: 9: def set_dispatcher(dispatcher) -> None: 10: global _dispatcher 11: _dispatcher = dispatcher 12: 13: 14: def get_valid_tasks(sb) -> list[str]: 15: result = sb.table("tasks").select("task_name").execute() 16: return [row["task_name"] for row in result.data] 17: 18: 19: def resolve_task_id(sb, task_name: str | None) -> str | None: 20: if not task_name: 21: return None 22: result = sb.table("tasks").select("id").eq("task_name", task_name).maybe_single().execute() 23: return result.data["id"] if result and result.data else None 24: 25: 26: def resolve_technician_id(sb, telegram_id) -> str | None: 27: if telegram_id is None: 28: return None 29: result = sb.table("technicians").select("id").eq("telegram_id", str(telegram_id)).maybe_single().execute() 30: return result.data["id"] if result and result.data else None 31: 32: 33: def save_original_message(sb, message: dict, telegram_id=None) -> str: 34: inserted = sb.table("original_messages").insert({ 35: "telegram_id": str(telegram_id) if telegram_id is not None else None, 36: "sender_name": message.get("sender_name"), 37: "chat_title": message.get("chat_title"), 38: "sent_at": message.get("sent_at"), 39: "type": message.get("type"), 40: "text": message.get("text"), 41: "replied_to": message.get("replied_to"), 42: }).execute() 43: return inserted.data[0]["id"] 44: 45: 46: def process_message(message: dict, telegram_id=None, dispatcher=None) -> dict | None: 47: sb = get_supabase_client() 48: original_message_id = save_original_message(sb, message, telegram_id) 49: 50: valid_tasks = get_valid_tasks(sb) 51: result = llm_analyze(message, valid_tasks) 52: if result is None: 53: return None 54: 55: sb.table("processed_messages").insert({ 56: "original_message_id": original_message_id, 57: "technician_id": resolve_technician_id(sb, telegram_id), 58: "task_id": resolve_task_id(sb, result.get("task_name")), 59: "label": result.get("label"), 60: "status": result.get("status"), 61: "material": result.get("material"), 62: "quantity": result.get("quantity"), 63: "confidence": CONFIDENCE_MAP.get(result.get("confidence")), 64: "note": result.get("note"), 65: "tier_resolved": "llm", 66: }).execute() 67: 68: task_id = resolve_task_id(sb, result.get("task_name")) 69: confidence = CONFIDENCE_MAP.get(result.get("confidence"), 0.5) 70: 71: if task_id and confidence == 1.0 and (dispatcher or _dispatcher): 72: label = result.get("label") 73: status = result.get("status") 74: technician_id = resolve_technician_id(sb, telegram_id) or "llm" 75: d = dispatcher or _dispatcher 76: 77: if label == "task_completion" and status == "done": 78: d.process_completion_report(task_id, technician_id) 79: elif label == "issue_report" or status == "blocked": 80: failure_type = result.get("note") or "SITE_NOT_READY" 81: d.process_failure_report(task_id, failure_type, technician_id) 82: elif label == "task_start" and status == "in_progress": 83: d.process_start_report(task_id, technician_id) 84: 85: return result 1: """ 2: Run all real-world-style tests in one go. 3: 4: Usage: 5: PYTHONPATH=. python -m backend.tests.run_all_realworld 6: 7: Exit code is non-zero if any crash or confirmed bug was found, so this 8: can be wired into a CI step or pre-commit hook later if desired. 9: """ 10: import sys 11: 12: from backend.tests import test_parser_realworld 13: from backend.tests import test_simulation_realworld 14: from backend.tests import test_p0_regression_cascade 15: from backend.tests import test_p0_custom_policy 16: from backend.tests import test_p1_schedule_pipeline 17: from backend.tests import test_p1_vrp_boundaries 18: from backend.tests import test_p2_edge_cases 19: 20: 21: def main(): 22: print("
" + "#" * 70) 23: print("# SYNCFIELD REAL-WORLD TEST SUITE (FULL)") 24: print("#" * 70) 25: 26: parser_result = test_parser_realworld.run() 27: cache_ok = test_parser_realworld.test_cache_set_arity_directly() 28: 29: sim_result = test_simulation_realworld.run() 30: 31: p0_regress = test_p0_regression_cascade.run() 32: p0_regress_partial = test_p0_regression_cascade.run_partial_regression() 33: 34: p0_policy = test_p0_custom_policy.run() 35: p0_policy_role = test_p0_custom_policy.run_role_check() 36: 37: p1_window = test_p1_schedule_pipeline.run_basic_window_removal() 38: p1_delay = test_p1_schedule_pipeline.run_delay_from() 39: p1_eta = test_p1_schedule_pipeline.run_eta_with_real_schedule() 40: 41: p1_skill = test_p1_vrp_boundaries.run_no_skill_match() 42: p1_inv = test_p1_vrp_boundaries.run_insufficient_inventory() 43: p1_shift = test_p1_vrp_boundaries.run_shift_overrun() 44: 45: p2_cycle = test_p2_edge_cases.run_cyclic_dependency() 46: p2_failed = test_p2_edge_cases.run_failed_task_reporting_failure() 47: 48: print("
" + "#" * 70) 49: print("# FINAL SUMMARY") 50: print("#" * 70) 51: print("--- Previously verified ---") 52: print(f"Parser crashes: {len(parser_result['crashes'])}") 53: print(f"resolution_cache.set() arity: {'OK' if cache_ok else 'BUG CONFIRMED'}") 54: print(f"All-COMPLETE ETA bug: {'BUG CONFIRMED' if sim_result['bug_reproduced'] else 'OK / fixed'}") 55: print("--- P0 ---") 56: print(f"Reverse cascade (COMPLETE->REGRESSED), multi-layer: {'PASS' if not p0_regress['failures'] else 'FAIL'}") 57: print(f"Reverse cascade, partial multi-parent regression: {'PASS' if p0_regress_partial['pass'] else 'FAIL'}") 58: print(f"Custom FailurePolicy retry count honored: {'PASS' if p0_policy['retries_ok'] else 'FAIL'}") 59: print(f"Custom FailurePolicy escalation role honored: {'PASS' if p0_policy_role['role_ok'] else 'FAIL'}") 60: print("--- P1 ---") 61: print(f"remove_window() excludes break correctly: {'PASS' if p1_window['pass'] else 'FAIL'}") 62: print(f"delay_from() shifts windows correctly: {'PASS' if p1_delay['pass'] else 'FAIL'}") 63: print(f"compute_cascade_eta() with real schedule: {'PASS' if p1_eta['pass'] else 'FAIL'}") 64: print(f"VRP: no skill match -> empty, no crash: {'PASS' if p1_skill['pass'] else 'FAIL'}") 65: print(f"VRP: insufficient inventory reason string correct: {'PASS' if p1_inv['pass'] else 'FAIL'}") 66: print(f"VRP: shift overrun + boundary correctly handled: {'PASS' if p1_shift['pass'] else 'FAIL'}") 67: print("--- P2 (documenting known gaps, not pass/fail) ---") 68: print(f"2-node cycle correctly rejected at construction: {'PASS' if p2_cycle['rejected'] else 'GAP'}") 69: print(f"FAILED re-report no-ops with ALREADY_FAILED: {'PASS' if p2_failed['guarded'] else 'GAP'}") 70: 71: failed = ( 72: bool(parser_result["crashes"]) or not cache_ok or sim_result["bug_reproduced"] 73: or p0_regress["failures"] or not p0_regress_partial["pass"] 74: or not p0_policy["retries_ok"] or not p0_policy_role["role_ok"] 75: or not p1_window["pass"] or not p1_delay["pass"] or not p1_eta["pass"] 76: or not p1_skill["pass"] or not p1_inv["pass"] or not p1_shift["pass"] 77: or not p2_cycle["rejected"] or not p2_failed["guarded"] 78: ) 79: 80: if failed: 81: print("
Result: ISSUES FOUND — see details above.") 82: sys.exit(1) 83: else: 84: print("
Result: ALL VERIFIED BEHAVIOR PASSES.") 85: print("Known gaps (documented, not crashing): cycle detection, FAILED re-report guard.") 86: sys.exit(0) 87: 88: 89: if __name__ == "__main__": 90: main() import Dict, List, Set, Tuple 4: from ortools.sat.python import cp_model 5: 6: 7: @dataclass 8: class Assignment: 9: technician_id: str 10: task_id: str 11: score: float 12: 13: 14: class VRPSolver: 15: def __init__(self, engine, technicians_db: Dict[str, dict]): 16: self.engine = engine 17: self.technicians = technicians_db 18: 19: @staticmethod 20: def _distance(tech: dict, task: dict) -> float: 21: t_lat = tech.get("lat") 22: t_lng = tech.get("lng") 23: task_lat = task.get("lat") 24: task_lng = task.get("lng") 25: if t_lat is None or t_lng is None or task_lat is None or task_lng is None: 26: return 0.0 27: return math.sqrt((t_lat - task_lat) ** 2 + (t_lng - task_lng) ** 2) 28: 29: def _is_eligible(self, tech: dict, task: dict, now_hour: float) -> Tuple[bool, str]: 30: required = set(task.get("required_skills", [])) 31: tech_skills = set(tech.get("skills", [])) 32: if required and not required.issubset(tech_skills): 33: missing = required - tech_skills 34: return False, f"skills_missing: {missing}" 35: 36: for item, qty in task.get("required_materials_qty", {}).items(): 37: available = tech.get("van_inventory", {}).get(item, 0) 38: if available < qty: 39: return False, f"insufficient_{item}: need {qty}, have {available}" 40: 41: duration = task.get("estimated_duration_hours", 2) 42: shift_end = tech.get("shift_end_hour", 18) 43: if now_hour + duration > shift_end: 44: return False, f"shift_overrun: need {duration}h, {shift_end - now_hour}h left" 45: 46: return True, "eligible" 47: 48: DISTANCE_PENALTY_WEIGHT = 5.0 49: OBJECTIVE_SCALE = 100 50: 51: def _pair_score(self, tech_id: str, task_id: str, tech: dict, task: dict) -> float: 52: priority = self.engine.compute_priority(task_id).score 53: distance_penalty = self._distance(tech, task) * self.DISTANCE_PENALTY_WEIGHT 54: return priority - distance_penalty 55: 56: def solve_reassignment( 57: self, idle_technician_ids: List[str], exclude_tasks: Set[str], now_hour: float = 9.0 58: ) -> List[Assignment]: 59: ready_tasks = { 60: t_id: t for t_id, t in self.engine.tasks.items() 61: if t["state"] == "READY" and t_id not in exclude_tasks and not t.get("assigned_to") 62: } 63: 64: if not ready_tasks or not idle_technician_ids: 65: return [] 66: 67: model = cp_model.CpModel() 68: x = {} 69: 70: for tech_id in idle_technician_ids: 71: tech = self.technicians.get(tech_id) 72: if tech is None: 73: continue 74: for task_id, task in ready_tasks.items(): 75: eligible, _ = self._is_eligible(tech, task, now_hour) 76: if eligible: 77: x[(tech_id, task_id)] = model.NewBoolVar(f"x_{tech_id}_{task_id}") 78: 79: if not x: 80: return [] 81: 82: for tech_id in idle_technician_ids: 83: vars_for_tech = [v for (t, _), v in x.items() if t == tech_id] 84: if vars_for_tech: 85: model.Add(sum(vars_for_tech) <= 1) 86: 87: for task_id in ready_tasks: 88: vars_for_task = [v for (_, k), v in x.items() if k == task_id] 89: if vars_for_task: 90: model.Add(sum(vars_for_task) <= 1) 91: 92: objective_terms = [] 93: pair_scores: Dict[Tuple[str, str], float] = {} 94: for (tech_id, task_id), var in x.items(): 95: tech = self.technicians[tech_id] 96: task = ready_tasks[task_id] 97: score = self._pair_score(tech_id, task_id, tech, task) 98: pair_scores[(tech_id, task_id)] = score 99: weight = int(score * self.OBJECTIVE_SCALE) 100: objective_terms.append(max(1, weight) * var) 101: 102: model.Maximize(sum(objective_terms)) 103: 104: solver = cp_model.CpSolver() 105: solver.parameters.max_time_in_seconds = 2.0 106: status = solver.Solve(model) 107: 108: results: List[Assignment] = [] 109: if status in (cp_model.OPTIMAL, cp_model.FEASIBLE): 110: for (tech_id, task_id), var in x.items(): 111: if solver.Value(var) == 1: 112: results.append(Assignment( 113: tech_id, task_id, round(pair_scores[(tech_id, task_id)], 2) 114: )) 115: 116: return results 1: from datetime import datetime, timedelta 2: 3: from backend.workflow.dag_engine.dag_engine import FieldOpsDomainRules, DEFAULT_POLICIES 4: 5: 6: class FieldOpsDispatcher: 7: def __init__(self, engine, solver, notifier, policies=None, sb_client=None): 8: self.engine = engine 9: self.solver = solver 10: self.notifier = notifier 11: self.domain_rules = FieldOpsDomainRules(policies or DEFAULT_POLICIES) 12: self.sb = sb_client 13: 14: def _sync(self, events: list) -> None: 15: if self.sb and events: 16: self.engine.sync_to_supabase(events, self.sb) 17: 18: def process_failure_report(self, task_id: str, failure_type: str, technician_id: str) -> "RETRY_LOCAL": 26: self.notifier.notify(technician_id, decision["msg"]) 27: return decision 28: 29: self.notifier.alert(role=decision["escalate_to_role"], payload=decision["msg"]) 30: 31: cascade_eta = self.engine.compute_cascade_eta(task_id) 32: decision["cascade_eta"] = cascade_eta 33: 34: for t in decision["affected_technicians"]: 35: t_id = t["task_id"] 36: eta = cascade_eta.get(t_id) 37: if eta: 38: t["eta_notification"] = self._build_eta_notification(t_id, eta) 39: 40: displaced_techs = [t["technician_id"] for t in decision["affected_technicians"]] 41: blocked_task_ids = {t["task_id"] for t in decision["affected_technicians"]} 42: 43: assignments = self.solver.solve_reassignment(displaced_techs, exclude_tasks=blocked_task_ids) 44: assigned_techs = {a.technician_id for a in assignments} 45: 46: for a in assignments: 47: self.engine.tasks[a.task_id]["assigned_to"] = a.technician_id 48: self.notifier.notify( 49: a.technician_id, 50: f"Rerouted to {a.task_id} (priority score {a.score:.1f})" 51: ) 52: 53: for tech_id in displaced_techs: 54: if tech_id not in assigned_techs: 55: self.notifier.notify(tech_id, "No eligible task available — standby.") 56: 57: for t in decision["affected_technicians"]: 58: if t["technician_id"] in assigned_techs: 59: self.engine.tasks[t["task_id"]]["assigned_to"] = None 60: 61: decision["rerouting_results"] = [ 62: {"technician_id": a.technician_id, "new_task": a.task_id} for a in assignments 63: ] 64: return decision 65: 66: @staticmethod 67: def _build_eta_notification(task_id: str, eta: dict) -> str: 68: confidence = eta.get("eta_confidence", "UNKNOWN") 69: start = eta.get("earliest_start") 70: finish = eta.get("earliest_finish") 71: 72: if confidence == "EXACT" and start: 73: return ( 74: f"Task {task_id} unblocked. " 75: f"Earliest start: {start.strftime('%d %b %H:%M')}." 76: ) 77: elif confidence == "ESTIMATED" and start: 78: return ( 79: f"Task {task_id} blocked. " 80: f"Estimated earliest start: ~{start.strftime('%d %b %H:%M')} " 81: f"(approximate — based on upstream estimates)." 82: ) 83: else: 84: return ( 85: f"Task {task_id} blocked. " 86: f"Cannot estimate start time — awaiting upstream resolution. " 87: f"You will be notified when status updates." 88: ) 89: 90: def process_start_report(self, task_id: str, technician_id: str) -> dict: 91: events = self.engine.update_task_state( 92: task_id, "ACTIVE", f"LLM: task start reported by {technician_id}" 93: ) 94: self._sync(events) 95: self.engine.tasks[task_id]["assigned_to"] = technician_id 96: self.notifier.notify( 97: technician_id, 98: f"Task {task_id} started — marked ACTIVE." 99: ) 100: return { 101: "task_id": task_id, 102: "technician_id": technician_id, 103: "events_triggered": len(events), 104: } 105: 106: def process_completion_report(self, task_id: str, technician_id: str) -> dict: 107: """ 108: Processes a manual or automated task completion. Updates DAG state 109: and triggers joint VRP reassignments for newly unlocked READY tasks. 110: """ 111: events = self.engine.update_task_state( 112: task_id, "COMPLETE", f"Completion reported by {technician_id}" 113: ) 114: self._sync(events) 115: 116: unlocked_task_ids = [ 117: e["task_id"] for e in events 118: if e["new_state"] == "READY" 119: ] 120: 121: active_assignments = { 122: t.get("assigned_to") for t in self.engine.tasks.values() 123: if t.get("assigned_to") and t["state"] in ("ACTIVE", "READY") 124: } 125: idle_tech_ids = [ 126: tech_id for tech_id in self.solver.technicians 127: if tech_id not in active_assignments 128: ] 129: 130: assignments = [] 131: if idle_tech_ids: 132: assignments = self.solver.solve_reassignment(idle_tech_ids, exclude_tasks=set()) 133: 134: for a in assignments: 135: self.engine.tasks[a.task_id]["assigned_to"] = a.technician_id 136: self.notifier.notify( 137: a.technician_id, 138: f"Task {a.task_id} unlocked and assigned (priority score {a.score:.1f})" 139: ) 140: 141: for tech_id in idle_tech_ids: 142: if tech_id not in {a.technician_id for a in assignments}: 143: self.notifier.notify(tech_id, "No eligible newly-unlocked task — standby.") 144: 145: return { 146: "task_id": task_id, 147: "events_triggered": len(events), 148: "unlocked_tasks": unlocked_task_ids, 149: "new_assignments": [ 150: {"technician_id": a.technician_id, "task_id": a.task_id} 151: for a in assignments 152: ], 153: } 154: 155: def handle_absence(self, technician_id: str, now_hour: float = 9.0) -> dict: 156: orphaned = [ 157: {"task_id": t_id, "state": t["state"]} 158: for t_id, t in self.engine.tasks.items() 159: if t.get("assigned_to") == technician_id 160: ] 161: 162: freed_ids: list = [] 163: all_events: list = [] 164: for task in orphaned: 165: t_id = task["task_id"] 166: self.engine.tasks[t_id]["assigned_to"] = None 167: if task["state"] == "ACTIVE": 168: events = self.engine.update_task_state( 169: t_id, "READY", f"Demoted from ACTIVE to READY — {technician_id} absent" 170: ) 171: all_events.extend(events) 172: freed_ids.append(t_id) 173: self._sync(all_events) 174: 175: all_idle_techs = [ 176: a.technician_id 190: self.notifier.notify( 191: a.technician_id, 192: f"Absorbed {a.task_id} (priority score {a.score:.1f})" 193: ) 194: 195: self.notifier.alert( 196: role="project_manager", 197: payload=( 198: f"Technician {technician_id} absent. FailurePolicy("TECHNICAL", "network_specialist", max_local_retries=1, urgency_weight=15.0), 25: "QUALITY": FailurePolicy("QUALITY", "site_supervisor", max_local_retries=0, urgency_weight=5.0), 26: } 27: 28: 29: @dataclass 30: class AvailabilityWindow: 31: start: datetime 32: end: datetime 33: source: str 34: confirmed: bool = True 35: 36: 37: class TechnicianSchedule: 38: def __init__(self, windows: List[AvailabilityWindow]): 39: self.windows = sorted(windows, key=lambda w: w.start) 40: 41: def next_available_slot(self, after: datetime, duration: timedelta) -> tuple[Optional[datetime], str]: 42: for w in self.windows: 43: if w.end <= after: 44: continue 45: slot_start = max(after, w.start) 46: if slot_start + duration <= w.end: 47: confidence = "EXACT" if w.confirmed else "ESTIMATED" 48: return slot_start, confidence 49: return None, "UNKNOWN" 50: 51: def remove_window(self, start: datetime, end: datetime, source: str = "unavailable") -> None: 52: new_windows = [] 53: for w in self.windows: 54: if w.end <= start or w.start >= end: 55: new_windows.append(w) 56: else: 57: if w.start < start: 58: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(w.start, start, w.source, w.confirmed)) 59: if w.end > end: 60: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(end, w.end, w.source, w.confirmed)) 61: self.windows = sorted(new_windows, key=lambda w: w.start) 62: 63: def delay_from(self, when: datetime, by: timedelta, confirmed: bool = False) -> None: 64: new_windows = [] 65: for w in self.windows: 66: if w.start >= when: 67: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(w.start + by, w.end + by, w.source, confirmed)) 68: elif w.start < when < w.end: 69: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(w.start, when, w.source, w.confirmed)) 70: new_windows.append(AvailabilityWindow(when + by, w.end + by, w.source, confirmed)) 71: else: 72: new_windows.append(w) 73: self.windows = sorted(new_windows, key=lambda w: w.start) 74: 75: # Define the explicit physical-world state space 76: VALID_STATES = { 77: "LOCKED", # Dependencies/preconditions not met 78: "READY", # Dependencies met, ready for dispatch 79: "ACTIVE", # Technician currently on-site working 80: "BLOCKED", # Started but halted by external issue (material/site) 81: "REGRESSED", # Was complete, but must be redone due to cascade/damage 82: "COMPLETE", # Work finished and verified 83: "FAILED", # Abandoned or cancelled 84: } 85: 86: # Strict transition matrix for direct manual/technician updates 87: VALID_MANUAL_TRANSITIONS = { 88: "LOCKED": {"READY"}, 89: "READY": {"ACTIVE", "LOCKED"}, 90: "ACTIVE": {"COMPLETE", "BLOCKED", "FAILED", "READY"}, 91: "BLOCKED": {"ACTIVE", "FAILED"}, 92: "COMPLETE": {"REGRESSED"}, 93: "REGRESSED": {"ACTIVE"}, 94: "FAILED": set(), 95: } 96: 97: MAX_CASCADE_DEPTH = 20 98: 99: @dataclass 100: class TaskPriority: 101: task_id: str 102: score: float 103: critical_path_len: int 104: days_to_deadline: Union[float, None] 105: hours_blocked: float 106: failure_category: Union[str, None] 107: granularity: str = "ESTIMATE" 108: data_sources_used: List[str] = None 109: 110: def __post_init__(self): 111: if self.data_sources_used is None: 112: self.data_sources_used = [] 113: 114: 115: class SyncFieldDAG: 116: def __init__(self, tasks_definition: List[dict]): 117: self.tasks: Dict[str, dict] = {t["task_id"]: t for t in tasks_definition} 118: self._precondition_hooks: Dict[str, List[Callable[[dict], bool]]] = {} 119: 120: for task in self.tasks.values(): 121: task.setdefault("state", "LOCKED") 122: task.setdefault("attempt_count", 0) 123: task.setdefault("failure_category", None) 124: task.setdefault("assigned_to", None) 125: task.setdefault("scheduled_start", None) 126: task.setdefault("created_at_epoch", datetime.now().timestamp()) 127: task.setdefault("resolution_eta", None) 128: task.setdefault("earliest_start", None) 129: task.setdefault("eta_confidence", "UNKNOWN") 130: 131: self.children_map: Dict[str, List[str]] = {t_id: [] for t_id in self.tasks} 132: for t_id, task in self.tasks.items(): 133: for dep in task.get("dependencies", []): 134: if dep in self.children_map: 135: self.children_map[dep].append(t_id) 136: 137: self._detect_cycles() 138: 139: self.evaluate_graph() 140: 141: def _detect_cycles(self) -> None: 142: visited: set = set() 143: rec_stack: set = set() 144: 145: def dfs(node: str) -> bool: 146: visited.add(node) 147: rec_stack.add(node) 148: for child in self.children_map.get(node, []): 149: if child not in visited: 150: if dfs(child): 151: return True 152: elif child in rec_stack: 153: return True 154: rec_stack.discard(node) 155: return False 156: 157: for t_id in self.tasks: 158: if t_id not in visited: 159: if dfs(t_id): 160: raise ValueError(f"Cycle detected in DAG — construction aborted") 161: 162: def get_descendants(self, start_node_id: str) -> Set[str]: 163: """Traverses downstream nodes using BFS to find all affected children.""" 164: descendants = set() 165: queue = [start_node_id] 166: while queue: 167: current = queue.pop(0) 168: for child in self.children_map.get(current, []): 169: if child not in descendants: 170: descendants.add(child) 171: queue.append(child) 172: return descendants 173: 174: def get_affected_technicians(self, blocked_task_id: str) -> List[dict]: 175: """Identifies every technician whose assigned work is downstream from a blocked node.""" 176: descendants = self.get_descendants(blocked_task_id) 177: affected = [] 178: 179: for t_id in descendants: 180: task = self.tasks[t_id] 181: if task.get("assigned_to"): 182: affected.append({ 183: "technician_id": task["assigned_to"], 184: "task_id": t_id, 185: "impact": "downstream_block", 186: "original_eta": task.get("scheduled_start") 187: }) 188: 189: direct_task = self.tasks.get(blocked_task_id) 190: if direct_task and direct_task.get("assigned_to"): 191: affected.append({ 192: "technician_id": direct_task["assigned_to"], 193: "task_id": blocked_task_id, 194: "impact": "direct_block", 195: "original_eta": direct_task.get("scheduled_start") 196: }) 197: 198: return affected 199: def compute_cascade_eta(self, blocked_task_id: str, schedules: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict: 200: """ 201: Propagates time impact downstream from a blocked node. 202: Confidence degrades: EXACT → ESTIMATED → UNKNOWN. 203: Optionally consults technician schedules for realistic start times. 204: """ 205: descendants = self.get_descendants(blocked_task_id) 206: order = [blocked_task_id] 207: queue = [blocked_task_id] 208: while queue: 209: current = queue.pop(0) 210: for child in self.children_map.get(current, []): 211: if child in descendants and child not in order: 212: order.append(child) 213: queue.append(child) 214: 215: results: dict = {} 216: 217: for t_id in order: 218: task = self.tasks[t_id] 219: deps = task.get("dependencies", []) 220: duration = timedelta(hours=task.get("estimated_duration_hours", 2)) 221: 222: if t_id == blocked_task_id: 223: if task.get("resolution_eta"): 224: results[t_id] = { 225: "earliest_start": task["resolution_eta"], 226: "earliest_finish": task["resolution_eta"] + duration, 227: "eta_confidence": task.get("eta_confidence", "EXACT"), 228: } 229: task["earliest_start"] = task["resolution_eta"] 230: task["eta_confidence"] = task.get("eta_confidence", "EXACT") 231: else: 232: results[t_id] = { 233: "earliest_start": None, 234: "earliest_finish": None, 235: "eta_confidence": "UNKNOWN", 236: "reason": "Blocked task has no resolution ETA", 237: } 238: task["earliest_start"] = None 239: task["eta_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN" 240: continue 241: 242: upstream_etas: list = [] 243: for dep in deps: 244: dep_task = self.tasks[dep] 245: if dep_task["state"] == "COMPLETE": 246: upstream_etas.append(("EXACT", datetime.min)) 247: elif dep_task.get("resolution_eta"): 248: dep_duration = dep_task.get("estimated_duration_hours", 2) 249: dep_finish = dep_task["resolution_eta"] + timedelta(hours=dep_duration) 250: upstream_etas.append(( 251: dep_task.get("eta_confidence", "ESTIMATED"), 252: dep_finish, 253: )) 254: else: 255: upstream_etas.append(("UNKNOWN", None)) 256: 257: any_unknown = any(conf == "UNKNOWN" for conf, _ in upstream_etas) if upstream_etas else False 258: any_estimated = any(conf == "ESTIMATED" for conf, _ in upstream_etas) if upstream_etas else False 259: 260: if any_unknown or not upstream_etas: 261: results[t_id] = { 262: "earliest_start": None, 263: "earliest_finish": None, 264: "eta_confidence": "UNKNOWN", 265: "reason": "Upstream resolution time not available" if any_unknown else "No dependency data", 266: } 267: task["earliest_start"] = None 268: task["eta_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN" 269: continue 270: 271: latest_upstream = max(eta for _, eta in upstream_etas if eta is not None) 272: assigned_tech = task.get("assigned_to") 273: 274: if schedules and assigned_tech and assigned_tech in schedules: 275: slot_start, slot_conf = schedules[assigned_tech].next_available_slot(latest_upstream, duration) 276: if slot_start is None: 277: results[t_id] = { 278: "earliest_start": None, 279: "earliest_finish": None, 280: "eta_confidence": "UNKNOWN", 281: "reason": f"{assigned_tech} has no available slot for {duration} from {latest_upstream}", 282: } 283: task["earliest_start"] = None 284: task["eta_confidence"] = "UNKNOWN" 285: continue 286: earliest_start = slot_start 287: effective_estimated = any_estimated or slot_conf == "ESTIMATED" 288: else: 289: earliest_start = latest_upstream 290: effective_estimated = any_estimated 291: 292: earliest_finish = earliest_start + duration 293: confidence = "ESTIMATED" if effective_estimated else "EXACT" 294: 295: results[t_id] = { 296: "earliest_start": earliest_start, 297: "earliest_finish": earliest_finish, 298: "eta_confidence": confidence, 299: } 300: task["earliest_start"] = earliest_start 301: task["eta_confidence"] = confidence 302: task["resolution_eta"] = earliest_start 303: 304: return results 305: 306: def register_precondition_hook(self, task_id: str, hook: Callable[[dict], bool]) -> None: 307: """Injects domain rules (e.g., inventory, site safety) into the generic engine.""" 308: if task_id not in self._precondition_hooks: 309: self._precondition_hooks[task_id] = [] 310: self._precondition_hooks[task_id].append(hook) 311: 312: def evaluate_graph(self, depth: int = 0, dirty: set = None) -> List[dict]: 313: """ 314: Maintains structural integrity by checking dependency states and hooks. 315: System cascades bypass the manual transition rules to enforce physical constraints. 316: Only READY tasks are re-locked — ACTIVE/BLOCKED/REGRESSED are protected. 317: """ 318: if depth >= MAX_CASCADE_DEPTH: 319: return [] 320: 321: candidates = dirty if dirty is not None else self.tasks.keys() 322: changes = [] 323: next_dirty = set() 324: 325: for t_id in candidates: 326: task = self.tasks[t_id] 327: current_state = task["state"] 328: 329: dependencies_met = all( 330: self.tasks[dep]["state"] == "COMPLETE" 331: for dep in task.get("dependencies", []) 332: ) 333: 334: hooks_passed = all( 335: hook(task) for hook in self._precondition_hooks.get(t_id, []) 336: ) 337: 338: if dependencies_met and hooks_passed: 339: if current_state == "LOCKED": 340: task["state"] = "READY" 341: changes.append({ 342: "task_id": t_id, 343: "old_state": "LOCKED", 344: "new_state": "READY", 345: "depth": depth, 346: "reason": "Dependencies and preconditions met" 347: }) 348: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 349: else: 350: if current_state == "READY": 351: task["state"] = "LOCKED" 352: changes.append({ 353: "task_id": t_id, 354: "old_state": "READY", 355: "new_state": "LOCKED", 356: "depth": depth, 357: "reason": "Prerequisites/preconditions no longer met" 358: }) 359: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 360: elif current_state == "BLOCKED": 361: task["state"] = "LOCKED" 362: if "blocked_since" in task: 363: del task["blocked_since"] 364: changes.append({ 365: "task_id": t_id, 366: "old_state": "BLOCKED", 367: "new_state": "LOCKED", 368: "depth": depth, 369: "reason": "Prerequisites no longer met — cleared block" 370: }) 371: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 372: elif current_state == "COMPLETE": 373: task["state"] = "REGRESSED" 374: changes.append({ 375: "task_id": t_id, 376: "old_state": "COMPLETE", 377: "new_state": "REGRESSED", 378: "depth": depth, 379: "reason": "Upstream dependency failed" 380: }) 381: next_dirty.update(self.children_map.get(t_id, [])) 382: 383: if changes: 384: changes.extend(self.evaluate_graph(depth + 1, dirty=next_dirty)) 385: 386: return changes 387: 388: def update_task_state(self, task_id: str, new_state: str, context: str = "") -> List[dict]: 389: """Updates a single task state manually while enforcing manual transition rules.""" 390: if task_id not in self.tasks: 391: raise ValueError(f"Task {task_id} not found.") 392: if new_state not in VALID_STATES: 393: raise ValueError(f"Invalid state: {new_state}") 394: 395: old_state = self.tasks[task_id]["state"] 396: if old_state == new_state: 397: return [] 398: 399: if new_state not in VALID_MANUAL_TRANSITIONS.get(old_state, set()): 400: raise ValueError(f"Illegal manual transition: {old_state} → {new_state} for task {task_id}") 401: 402: self.tasks[task_id]["state"] = new_state 403: 404: if new_state == "COMPLETE": 405: self.tasks[task_id]["attempt_count"] = 0 406: self.tasks[task_id]["failure_category"] = None 407: 408: if new_state == "BLOCKED": 409: self.tasks[task_id]["blocked_since"] = datetime.now() 410: elif old_state == "BLOCKED" and "blocked_since" in self.tasks[task_id]: 411: del self.tasks[task_id]["blocked_since"] 412: 413: print(f"
⚡ State Transition: [{task_id}] manually updated {old_state} ➞ {new_state} ({context})") 414: 415: events = [{"task_id": task_id, "old_state": old_state, "new_state": new_state, "depth": 0, "reason": context or "Manual operator update"}] 416: cascade_events = self.evaluate_graph(depth=0) 417: events.extend(cascade_events) 418: 419: return events 420: 421: def compute_priority(self, task_id: str) -> TaskPriority: 422: """Calculates a multi-factor priority score to guide allocation decisions.""" 423: task = self.tasks[task_id] 424: score = 0.0 425: data_sources: List[str] = [] 426: 427: # Factor 1: Critical path length 428: descendants = self.get_descendants(task_id) 429: critical_path_score = len(descendants) * 10.0 430: score += critical_path_score 431: data_sources.append("dag_topology") 432: 433: # Factor 2: Time pressure / Deadline proximity 434: deadline_score = 0.0 435: days_left = None 436: if "deadline" in task: 437: days_left = (task["deadline"] - datetime.now()).total_seconds() / 86400.0 438: deadline_score = max(0.0, (7.0 - days_left) * 5.0) 439: score += deadline_score 440: data_sources.append("deadline") 441: 442: # Factor 3: Resource contention (shared tooling) 443: contention_score = 8.0 if task.get("requires_shared_tool") else 0.0 444: score += contention_score 445: if task.get("requires_shared_tool"): 446: data_sources.append("inventory") 447: 448: # Factor 4: Blocker age 449: hours_blocked = 0.0 450: blocker_score = 0.0 451: if task["state"] == "BLOCKED" and "blocked_since" in task: 452: hours_blocked = (datetime.now() - task["blocked_since"]).total_seconds() / 3600.0 453: blocker_score = hours_blocked * 2.0 454: score += blocker_score 455: data_sources.append("blocked_since") 456: 457: # Factor 5: Chronological tiebreaker 458: tiebreaker = 1.0 / (task.get("created_at_epoch", 1.0)) 459: score += tiebreaker 460: data_sources.append("created_at") 461: 462: # Factor 6: Failure category urgency weight 463: category = task.get("failure_category") 464: category_weight = DEFAULT_POLICIES[category].urgency_weight if category and category in DEFAULT_POLICIES else 0.0 465: score += category_weight 466: if category: 467: data_sources.append("failure_category") 468: 469: granularity = "DETAILED" if "deadline" in task and "estimated_duration_hours" in task else "ESTIMATE" 470: 471: return TaskPriority( 472: task_id=task_id, 473: score=round(score, 2), 474: critical_path_len=len(descendants), 475: days_to_deadline=round(days_left, 1) if days_left is not None else None, 476: hours_blocked=round(hours_blocked, 1), 477: failure_category=category, 478: granularity=granularity, 479: data_sources_used=data_sources, 480: ) 481: 482: def print_state(self): 483: print("-" * 80) 484: print(f"{'ID':<5} | {'Task Name':<38} | {'State':<12} | {'Priority Score'}") 485: print("-" * 80) 486: for t_id, task in self.tasks.items(): 487: p = self.compute_priority(t_id) 488: print(f"{t_id:<5} | {task['task_name']:<38} | {task['state']:<12} | {p.score} (Blocker hr: {p.hours_blocked})") 489: print("-" * 80) 490: 491: def sync_to_supabase(self, events: List[dict], sb_client) -> None: 492: if not events: 493: return 494: 495: seen_tasks: dict = {} 496: for event in events: 497: seen_tasks[event["task_id"]] = event 498: 499: task_upserts = [] 500: for task_id, event in seen_tasks.items(): 501: task = self.tasks[task_id] 502: earliest = task.get("earliest_start") 503: task_upserts.append({ 504: "id": task_id, 505: "state": event["new_state"], 506: "failure_category": task.get("failure_category"), 507: "attempt_count": task.get("attempt_count", 0), 508: "earliest_start": earliest.isoformat() if earliest else None, 509: "eta_confidence": task.get("eta_confidence", "UNKNOWN"), 510: "updated_at": "now()", 511: }) 512: 513: event_inserts = [{ 514: "task_id": e["task_id"], 515: "old_state": e["old_state"], 516: "new_state": e["new_state"], 517: "reason": e["reason"], 518: "triggered_by": self._triggered_by(e), 519: "depth": e.get("depth", 0), 520: } for e in events] 521: 522: if task_upserts: 523: try: 524: sb_client.table("tasks").upsert(task_upserts).execute() 525: except Exception: 526: stripped = [] 527: for row in task_upserts: 528: stripped.append({k: v for k, v in row.items() 529: if k in ("id", "state", "failure_category", 530: "attempt_count", "updated_at")}) 531: sb_client.table("tasks").upsert(stripped).execute() 532: if event_inserts: 533: sb_client.table("task_events").insert(event_inserts).execute() 534: 535: @staticmethod 536: def _triggered_by(event: dict) -> str: 537: reason = event.get("reason", "") 538: if "Dependencies and preconditions met" in reason: 539: return "system" 540: if reason == "Manual operator update": 541: return "manual" 542: return "cascade" 543: 544: 545: class FieldOpsDomainRules: 546: def __init__(self, policies: dict[str, FailurePolicy] = None): 547: self.policies = policies or DEFAULT_POLICIES 548: 549: @staticmethod 550: def find_failure_category(failure_type: str) -> str: 551: for category, types in FAILURE_CATEGORIES.items(): 552: if failure_type in types: 553: return category 554: raise ValueError(f"Unknown failure type: {failure_type}") 555: 556: def handle_task_failure(self, engine: SyncFieldDAG, task_id: str, failure_type: str, technician_id: str) -> dict: 557: task = engine.tasks[task_id] 558: 559: if task["state"] == "FAILED": 560: return { 561: "action": "ALREADY_FAILED", 562: "assignee": technician_id, 563: "msg": f"Task {task_id} is already FAILED — ignoring late failure report." 564: } 565: 566: category = self.find_failure_category(failure_type) 567: policy = self.policies.get(category) 568: if not policy: 569: raise ValueError(f"No policy for failure category: {category}") 570: 571: task["failure_category"] = category 572: task["attempt_count"] += 1 573: attempt = task["attempt_count"] 574: 575: if attempt <= policy.max_local_retries: 576: events = engine.update_task_state(task_id, "BLOCKED", f"Local retry {attempt}/{policy.max_local_retries} for {failure_type}") 577: return { 578: "action": "RETRY_LOCAL", 579: "assignee": technician_id, 580: "msg": f"Technical issue ({failure_type}). Retrying task locally. Attempt {attempt}/{policy.max_local_retries}.", 581: "_events": events, 582: } 583: else: 584: events = engine.update_task_state(task_id, "BLOCKED", f"Escalated to {policy.escalate_to_role} due to {failure_type}") 585: affected_techs = engine.get_affected_technicians(task_id) 586: return { 587: "action": "ESCALATE", 588: "assigned_to": technician_id, 589: "escalate_to_role": policy.escalate_to_role, 590: "affected_technicians": affected_techs, 591: "msg": f"Escalated {failure_type} to {policy.escalate_to_role}. Blocked downstream path contains {len(affected_techs)} staff.", 592: "_events": events, 593: }
repomix.instruction:
<!-- BEGIN:nextjs-agent-rules --> # This is NOT the Next.js you know
This version has breaking changes — APIs, conventions, and file structure may all differ from your training data. Read the relevant guide in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing any code. Heed deprecation notices. <!-- END:nextjs-agent-rules -->
## Quick start
```sh npm run dev # http://localhost:3000 npm run build # production build npm run lint # ESLint v9 flat config (not `next lint`) npm start # serve production build ```
## Stack
- **Next.js 16** + **React 19** — read docs in `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before coding - **TypeScript** — `@/` maps to `./src/*` - **Tailwind v4** — uses `@import "tailwindcss"` (not `@tailwind` directives) - **React Compiler** enabled in `next.config.ts` (needs `babel-plugin-react-compiler`)
## Auth
- **better-auth** with Google OAuth + Prisma adapter + PostgreSQL - Server: `src/lib/auth.ts` — client: `src/lib/auth-client.ts` - API route: `src/app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts` - Middleware: root-level `proxy.ts` (not `src/middleware.ts`) — protects `/dashboard`
## Prisma v7
- Config: `prisma.config.ts` (new v7 format, not the old `prisma` CLI default) - Client generated to `src/generated/prisma/client` (custom path, gitignored) - Run `npx prisma generate` before first build; run `npx prisma migrate dev` for schema changes - Requires `DATABASE_URL` env var (loaded via `dotenv` in `prisma.config.ts`)
## Environment
Required vars: `DATABASE_URL`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `BETTER_AUTH_URL` Required (Supabase): `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` (Python), `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (frontend)
**`.env` contains live credentials** (Telegram bot token, Google OAuth secrets) — never log or commit them.
## Workflow
- **Never push directly to `main`** — always create a PR from a feature branch.
## Caveats
- No test framework installed — skip any test commands - No CI pipeline or `.github/` directory - Python/Telegram bot artifacts present (`bot_session.session`, `__pycache__/`) but unrelated to the Next.js app
